response.extracted_text: The Scale of Waste Waste Category Administrative Complexity Pricing Failure Care Delivery Failure Overtreatment Fraud & Abuse Care Coordination Failure 265 230 165 105 85 80 0 50 100 150 200 250 Estimated Annual Waste ($B) Dollars That Never Reach Patients The National Academy of Medicine estimates that $760 billion to over $1 trillion in annual U.S. healthcare spending is wasted. Administrative complexity alone accounts for the single largest category — costs absorbed by documentation, billing, and compliance rather than care. This is not inefficiency at the margins. It is a structural feature of how the system is built — and a target for clinician-led redesign.
response.layout_description: A large dark-blue title sits at the upper left. The lower-left half contains a horizontal bar chart, while the right half contains a bold heading and two large explanatory text paragraphs.
text_blocks: Waste Category Administrative Complexity Pricing Failure Care Delivery Failure Overtreatment Fraud & Abuse Care Coordination Failure 265 230 165 105 85 80 0 50 100 150 200 250 Estimated Annual Waste ($B)

The National Academy of Medicine estimates that $760 billion to over $1 trillion in annual U.S. healthcare spending is wasted. Administrative complexity alone accounts for the single largest category — costs absorbed by documentation, billing, and compliance rather than care.
This is not inefficiency at the margins. It is a structural feature of how the system is built — and a target for clinician-led redesign.

visual_elements.text: The National Academy of Medicine estimates that $760 billion to over $1 trillion in annual U.S. healthcare spending is wasted. Administrative complexity alone accounts for the single largest category — costs absorbed by documentation, billing, and compliance rather than care. This is not inefficiency at the margins. It is a structural feature of how the system is built — and a target for clinician-led redesign.
